content.problemStatement:
Quick! You need to solve this problem in order to help the Chef get an A on a math exam! The problem is as follows:

You are given two integers: XXX and MMM. Now, you need to replace each digit of XXX (let‚Äôs call them did_idi‚Äč) with diMmod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ10d_i^M \mod 10diM‚Äčmod10. Let‚Äôs call the new number YYY and reverse it (explained in note 3). Check whether YYY is divisible by 777.

Note 1:  diM=di‚čÖdi‚čÖ...‚čÖdid_i^M = d_i \cdot d_i \cdot ... \cdot d_idiM‚Äč=di‚Äč‚čÖdi‚Äč‚čÖ...‚čÖdi‚Äč (MMM times) - definition of did_idi‚Äč to the power of MMM. Special case: diM=1d_i^M = 1diM‚Äč=1, when M=0M = 0M=0

Note 2: Xmod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ10X \mod 10Xmod10 is the remainder of division XXX by 101010, where XXX is some integer number.

Note 3: Reversing an integer is an operation that reverse the order of its digits and erase leading zeros. For example: 123123123 when reversed becomes 321321321 and 450450450 when reversed becomes 545454.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer: TTT - representing the number of test cases
Each of the test cases contains a line with two space-separated integers: XXX and MMM


Output Format


For each test case output a line containing either ‚ÄúYES‚ÄĚ or ‚ÄúNO‚ÄĚ depending on whether YYY is divisible by 777.




You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example: the strings ‚Äúyes‚ÄĚ, ‚ÄúYeS‚ÄĚ, ‚ÄúYES‚ÄĚ will be treated as the same strings).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
After squaring all the digits the number we get is 149149149 and when we reverse it, it becomes 941941941. 941941941 is not divisible by 777.

Explanation:
111 to the power of 444 is 111. 222 to the power of 444 is 16mod‚ÄČ‚ÄČ10=616 \mod 10 = 616mod10=6. 333 to the power of 444 modulo 101010 is 111. So the number we get is 161161161 and it stays the same when reversed. 161161161 is divisible by 777.
